LAW AND MOTION 302, THE FIRST TWO CLAIMS ARE SEVERED (NOT BIFURCATED) FROM THE THIRD CLAIM IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY MR. CORBETT ON APRIL 11, 2017 AND THOSE TWO CLAIMS ARE TRANSFERRED TO THE CALIFORNIA SUPREME COURT. BOTH SIDES AGREE THAT THIS COURT LACKS JURISDICTION OVER THE FIRST TWO CLAIMS AND THAT THE CALIFORNIA SUPREME COURT HAS JURISDICTION OVER THOSE CLAIMS. UNDER THESE CIRCUMSTANCES, CCP 396(B) MAKES IT MANDATORY TO TRANSFER THOSE CLAIMS TO THE CALIFORNIA SUPREME COURT. ANY COSTS OF TRANSFER ARE TO BE PAID BY MR. CORBETT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189

DECLARATION OF DESTIE LEE OVERPECK IN SUPPORT OF OPPOSITION TO PLAINTIFF MOTION TO BIFURCATE AND TRANSFER PART OF ACTION (TRANSACTION ID # 60553236) FILED BY DEFENDANT STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, AS TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF THE CALIFORNIA PUBLIC RECORDS ACT IN PLAINTIFF JONATHAN CORBETT'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, IS TREATED AS A MOTION TO STRIKE BECAUSE THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION ALLEGES TWO DISTINCT REQUESTS FOR DOCUMENTS. THE TWO REQUESTS ARE FOR: 1) "DOCUMENTS THAT EXPLAIN HOW AND WHY THE BAR COMES UP WITH THEIR SCALING/RAW SCORE FORMULAE" AND 2) "REDACTED AND ANONYMIZED COPIES OF ALL SCORE REPORTS FOR 2 PRIOR SITTINGS OF THE FYLSX EXAM." (FAC 28). THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS GRANTED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FIRST SET OF REQUESTED DOCUMENTS AND DENIED AS TO THE SECOND SET OF REQUESTED DOCUMENTS. THE FIRST REQUEST SEEKS DOCUMENTS THAT CONSTITUTE "OTHER EXAMINATION DATA USED TO ADMINISTER A LICENSING EXAMINATION" PER GOVERNMENT CODE 6354(G) AND ARE THUS EXEMPT FROM DISCLOSURE. THE SECOND SET OF REQUESTED DOCUMENTS IS NOT NECESSARILY BARRED BY BUSINESS AND PROFESSIONS CODE 6060.25, WHICH ONLY EXEMPTS INFORMATION THAT "MAY IDENTIFY AN INDIVIDUAL." WHILE THE STATE BAR ARGUES THAT THE SCORES THEMSELVES WILL LEAD TO IDENTIFICATION, THAT IS A FACTUAL ISSUE THAT CANNOT BE RESOLVED ON A MOTION TO STRIKE OR DEMURRER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR #8189

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Feb-23-2018 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-28-2017 Set For Trial On Mar-12-18 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-14-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - JONATHAN CORBETT, TEL. NO. 1-305-600-0410, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. DESTIE L. OVERPECK, ASST. GENERAL COUNSEL, TEL. NO. 1-415-538-2333 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT STATE BAR OF CALIFORNIA'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to the third cause of action for violation of the California Public Records Act in plaintiff Jonathan Corbett's first amended Complaint, is treated as a motion to strike because the third cause of action alleges two distinct requests for documents. The two requests are for: 1) "documents that explain how and why the Bar comes up with their scaling/raw score formulae" and 2) "redacted and anonymized copies of all score reports for 2 prior sittings of the FYLSX exam." (FAC 28). The motion to strike is GRANTED without leave to amend as to the first set of requested documents and denied as to the second set of requested documents. The first request seeks documents that constitute "other examination data used to administer a licensing examination" per Government Code 6354(g) and are thus exempt from disclosure. The second set of requested documents is not necessarily barred by Business and Professions Code 6060.25, which only exempts information that "may identify an individual." While the State Bar argues that the scores themselves will lead to identification, that is a factual issue that cannot be resolved on a motion to strike or demurrer. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR #8189

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Bifurcate And Transfer Part Of Action With Incorporated Memorandum Of Law
Off Calendar May-17-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - JONATHAN CORBETT, TEL. NO. 1-305-600-0410, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE;
ATTY. ROBERT G. RETANA, DEPUTY GENERAL COUNSEL, TEL. NO. 1-415-538-2337 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: The first two claims are severed (not bifurcated) from the third claim in the first amended complaint filed by Mr. Corbett on April 11, 2017 and those two claims are transferred to the California Supreme Court. Both sides agree that this court lacks jurisdiction over the first two claims and that the California Supreme Court has jurisdiction over those claims. Under these circumstances, CCP 396(b) makes it mandatory to transfer those claims to the California Supreme Court. Any costs of transfer are to be paid by Mr. Corbett. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189
